rationale: PR is a frontend LCP performance optimization that matches the body's claims: apps/web/index.html removes Google Fonts preconnect+preload+stylesheet+noscript chain, the unsplash poster preload is downsized from w=1920 to w=1200&h=675&q=75 and gains fetchpriority='high'. apps/web/src/App.tsx imports Home eagerly (no longer lazy) and replaces three engagement widgets (AssistWidget, PlanosEngagementBanner, ExitIntentModal) with a single DeferredEngagement wrapper. The new DeferredEngagement.tsx lazy-loads them and defers mount via runAfterFirstPaint(setReady, 400). HeroVideo.tsx adds deferVideo prop (default true), gates <video> rendering on shouldPlayVideo, and uses runWhenIdle() to set it after 1800ms. main.tsx moves initTracking() into runAfterFirstPaint(800) and wraps the production SW register in runAfterFirstPaint(1200). Home.tsx removes framer-motion useScroll/useTransform/motion wrappers in favor of CSS `hero-enter` classes with delay-1..delay-6 keyframes added to index.css, with a prefers-reduced-motion override. media.ts adds HERO_POSTER_LCP (1200x675) and HERO_POSTER_SRCSET. Two empty `catch (){...}` blocks exist (one Pexels fallback comment, one /* ignore */ around sessionStorage) but both are bounded graceful-degradation patterns common in browser perf code, not error-swallow cheats hiding real bugs.
